name: HMS Falk (FY 789)
events: 16 Oct 1941 Around 1015 B/16, HMS Triumph (Cdr. W.J.W. Woods, RN) departed from Alexandria for her 19th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Aegean. She is also to perform two special operations.

On departure a practice attack was made during which HMS Falk (Lt. H.S. Upperton, RNR) acted as target. This attack was followed by A/S exercises with HMS Falk and HMS Kingston Coral (Ch.Skr. I. Mackintosh, MBE, RNR). (1)
5 Nov 1941 The A/S whaler HMS KOS XIX / Cocker (Lt. L.H. Davies, RNR) sighted a periscope about 32 nautical miles west of Alexandria at 2220/5.
A hunt was started and she was joined by the corvette HMS Snapdragon (T/Lt. P.H. Potter, RNR), A/S whaler HMS Falk (Lt. H.S. Upperton, RNR) and the A/S motor boat HMS MA/SB 2.
Destroyer HMS Encounter (Lt.Cdr. E.V.St J. Morgan, RN) was sent from Alexandria to assist. She already sailed at 2325/5.
Two more destroyers, HMS Hotspur (Lt. T.D. Herrick, DSC, RN) and HMS Decoy (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Alliston, RN), joined in the morning 6th. They had sailed at 0900/6 and 1000/6 respectively.
The destroyers were recalled to harbour at 1800 and arrived later in the evening. No contact had been obtained.

As an oil slick was sighted in the area of the hunt on the 7th the destroyers HMS Hotspur and HMS Kipling (Cdr. A. St.Clair-Ford, RN) were sent to hunt in the area again. They departed Alexandria at 1200 and 1400 hours respectively.
The search continued during the night of 7/8 November. HMS Encounter joined the destroyers in the morning. She sailed from Alexandria at 0815/8.

The hunt was however soon abandoned as no contact could be obtained and and the three destroyers then escorted the light cruiser HMS Neptune (Capt. R.C. O'Conor, RN) during a practice bombardment at Aboukir. HMS Neptune had departed Alexandria at 1000/8.
Following the exercise HMS Neptune immediately returned to Alexandria arriving at 1630/8.
HMS Encounter, HMS Hotspur and HMS Kipling returned to Alexandria at 1015/9 having remained outside on patrol for another night. (2)
4 Apr 1942 HMS Otus (Lt. R.M. Favell, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Alexandria together with HMS Klo (Lt. M.C. English, RNR) and HMS Falk (Lt. H.S. Upperton, RNR). (3)

14 Apr 1942 HMS Otus (Lt. R.M. Favell, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Alexandria together with HMS Hero (Cdr. H.W. Biggs, DSO and Bar, RN), HMS Peony (Lt.Cdr.(ret.) M.B. Sherwood, DSO and Bar, RN), HMS Gloxinia (Lt. A.F. Harkness, DSC, RNR), HMS Hurworth (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, RN), HMS Dulverton (Lt.Cdr. W.N. Petch, OBE, RN), HMS Falk (Lt. H.S. Upperton, RNR) and HMS Klo (Lt. M.C. English, RNR). HMS Dulverton (Lt.Cdr. W.N. Petch, OBE, RN) (3)
This convoy departed Port Said at 1130 A on 12 November 1943.

On departure from Port Said this convoy was made up of the following merchant vessels; Buteshire (British, 6590 GRT, built 1912), City of Derby (British, 6616 GRT, built 1921), Comedian (British, 5122 GRT, built 1929), Coulgorm (British, 6997 GRT, built 1942), Empire Voice (British, 6828 GRT, built 1940), Jean L.D. (French, 5795 GRT, built 1935), Magician (British, 5105 GRT, built 1925), Rodsley (British, 5000 GRT, built 1939), Sampa (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and Samshire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943).
On departure from Port Said the convoy was escorted by the corvette HMS Alisma (Lt. G. Lanning, RANVR) and the A/S whaler HMS Falk (T/Lt. W. Metcalfe, RNVR).

At 0830 A/13, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Alexandria; Defender (British, 8078 GRT, built 1915), Director (British, 5107 GRT, built 1926), Empire Falstaff (British, 7067 GRT, built 1943), Empire Farmer (British, 7049 GRT, built 1943), Fort Yale (British, 7132 GRT, built 1942), Magdala (Dutch (tanker), 8248 GRT, built 1931), Samarina (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and Samphire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943).
The Defender returned to Alexandria with engine defects though.
They were escorted by the frigate HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. N.K. Boyd, DSC and Bar, RNR), escort destroyer Miaoulis (?) and the corvette HMS Spiraea (Lt. A.H. Pierce, OBE, RNR).
At 1640 A/14, the minesweeper HMS Whitehaven (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) G.W.A.T. Irvine, DSC, RNR) joined the convoy coming from Benghazi.
[We have been unable to find out when HMS Falk parted company with the convoy and returned to Port Said. Most likely she was with the convoy only rather briefly.]
[We have also been unable to find out when HMS Whitehaven parted company with the convoy.]

At 1200 A/17, in position 36°29'N, 15°43'E, the following merchant vessels were detached to Augusta; Comedian, Director, Empire Falstaff, Empire Farmer, Fort Yale, Samarina, Sampa, Samphire and Samshire.

At 1330 A/17, in position 36°31'N, 15°26'E, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Amos Kendall (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Andrea Gritti (Italian, 6404 GRT, built 1943), Cap Padaran (British (former French), 8009 GRT, built 1922), Empire Darwin (British, 6765 GRT, built 1941), Empire Zephyr (British, 6327 GRT, built 1941), Fort Ash (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Fort Carillon (British, 7129 GRT, built 1943), Fort Gibraltar (British, 7134 GRT, built 1942), Fort Gloucester (British, 7127 GRT, built 1943), Fort Grahame (British, 7133 GRT, built 1943), Fort Souris (British, 7134 GRT, built 1942), Fort Wellington (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Francis Marion (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Glaisdale (British, 3777 GRT, built 1929), Ile de Brehat (French, 6176 GRT, built 1919), Indiana (Panamanian (former French), 5751 GRT, built 1917), James G. Birney (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), James J. Hill (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), James J. Pettigrew (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), James Whitcomb Riley (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), John Rutledge (American, 7181 GRT, built 1942), John W. Brown (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), M. M. Guhin (American, 7180 GRT, built 1943), Marigot (French, 4047 GRT, built 1932), Ocean Faith (British, 7174 GRT, built 1942), Pocahontas (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942), Shickshinny (American, 5103 GRT, built 1919), Thaddeus Kosciuszko (American, 7200 GRT, built 1942), Thorshov (Norwegian (tanker), 9955 GRT, built 1935) and Toltec (Honduran, 5527 GRT, built 1929).

At 1630 A/17, in position 36°28'N, 14°56'E, the following merchant vessels were detached to Malta; Cap Padaran and Rodsley while the following vessels joined the convoy coming from Malta; The submarine HMS Unrivalled (Lt. H.B. Turner, DSC, RN), corvettes HMS Honeysuckle (Lt. H.H.D. MacKillican, DSC and Bar, RNR), HMS Oxlip (Lt. C.W. Leadbetter, RNR) and the A/S trawler HMS Retriever (T/A/Lt.Cdr. G.E. Geeve, RNR). The corvette HMS Jonquil (T/Lt. R.W. Tretheway, RNR) had also come with these ships from Malta but she did not join the convoy but escorted the two merchant vessels that parted company with the convoy to Malta.

At 2200 A/18, off Bizerta the following merchant vessels parted company with the convoy; Amos Kendall, Empire Zephyr. Fort Carillon, Fort Souris, Francis Marion, James G. Birney, James J. Hill, James Whitcomb Riley, John Rutledge, Shickshinny, Thaddeus Kosciuszko, Thorshov and Toltec while the following merchant vessels joined the convoy; Atlantic Coast (British, 890 GRT, built 1934), Benjamin Tay (British, 1814 GRT, built 1943), Chloris British, 1171 GRT, built 1910), Empire Gareth (British, 2847 GRT, built 1942), Empire Newton (British, 7037 GRT, built 1942), Lochee (British, 964 GRT, built 1937), Ocean Vengeance (British, 7174 GRT, built 1942) and Pilar de Larrinaga (British, 7352 GRT, built 1918). Also the AA ship HMS Palomares (A/Capt.(Retd.) J.H. Jauncey, RN) reinforced the escort of the convoy.

At 1200 A/19, in position 37°17'N, 07°59'E, the following merchant vessels parted company with the convoy to proceed to Bone; Fort Ash and while the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bone; Danio (Norwegian, 3496 GRT, built 1924), Fort Grant (British, 7133 GRT, built 1943), Kelbergen (Dutch, 4823 GRT, built 1914) and Ocean Fame (British, 7173 GRT, built 1942). Also the escort destroyer HMS Calpe (Lt.Cdr. H. Kirkwood, DSC, RN) joined the convoy as additional escort.

At 1700 A/19, off Philippeville, the merchant vessels Chloris, Fort Grant and Ocean Fame parted company with the convoy while the merchant vessel George C. Childress (American, 7245 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy. The Atlantic Coast also put into Philippeville, apparently having straggled from the convoy.

At 0400 A/20, off Bougie, the merchant vessels Benjamin Tay, Empire Newton, Glaisdale, Ocean Vengeance and Ocean Faith parted company with the convoy. [According to the Commodore report only two merchant vessels parted company with the convoy off Bougie but the merchant vessel shipping cards of these six ship indicated that they all went to Bougie.]

At 0700 A/21, off Algiers, the merchant vessels Andrea Gritti, Fort Gibraltar, Fort Gloucester, George C. Childress, Lochee, Magdala and Marigot parted company with the convoy as did the escort destroyer RHS Miaoulis. The merchant vessel Indiana apparently also put into Algiers after having straggled from the convoy. The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Algiers; Cochrane (British, 7203 GRT, built 1923), Empire Scout (British, 2185 GRT, built 1936), Empire Snipe (British, 2497 GRT, built 1919), Ninella (British (tanker), 8134 GRT, built 1943), Thraki (Greek, 7460 GRT, built 1941) and Topsdalfjord (Norwegian, 4271 GRT, built 1921). The Empire Scout however returned to Algiers with engine trouble.

At 0900 A/22, off Oran the following merchant vessels parted company with the convoy; Ile de Brehat, James J. Pettigrew, John W. Brown, M. M. Guhin and Pocahontas while the merchant vessels Eskdalegate (British, 4250 GRT, built 1930), Pendeen (British, 4174 GRT, built 1923) and Slemmestad (Norwegian, 4258 GRT, built 1928) joined the convoy coming from Oran.

Early in the afternoon twelve USN LST's also joined the convoy, this were the following; USS LST-2 (T/Lt. W.G. Newhard, USN), USS LST-308 (Lt.Cdr. S.B. Purdie, USNR), USS LST-311 (Lt.Cdr. R.L. Coleman, USNR), USS LST-312 (T/Lt. C.L. Haslup, USN), USS LST-344 (T/Lt. R.B. Hensley, USN), USS LST-345 (Lt.Cdr. D.H. Adams, USNR), USS LST-346 (Lt. J.Z. Howell, USNR), USS LST-370 (Lt.Cdr. D.M. Hicks, USNR), USS LST-371 (T/Lt. A.W. Melohusky, USN), USS LST-380 (Lt. M.A. Goodrich, USNR), USS LST-382 (Lt.(jg) B.E. Toon, USNR) and USS LST-400 (Lt. C.J. Lyden, USNR).

Around 1445 A/23, the following merchant vessel parted company with the convoy; Cochrane, Empire Darwin, Fort Grahame, Fort Wellington, Ninella, Thraki and Topsdalfjord. Also parting company were HMS Unrivalled and HMS Palomares.
HMS Honeysuckle and HMS Oxlip also parted company to refuel at Gibraltar on completion of which they were to rejoin the convoy which after passing Gibraltar would be known as convoy MKS 31(G).

The remainder of the convoy, now known as convoy MKS 31(G), continued into the Atlantic, with the addition of the Gibraltar section of convoy MKS 31(G), to make rendezvous with convoy SL 141 coming from Freetown after which the convoy would continue to the UK as the combined convoy SL 141 / MKS 31(G). (4)
17 Jan 1944 HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. W.J. de Vries, RNN) departed Malta for Gibraltar.
She joined convoy KMF 27A for the passage. O 24 was escorted to the rendezvous with the convoy by HMS Falk (T/Lt. W. Metcalfe, RNVR).
[For more info on this convoy see the event ' Convoy MKF 27A ' for 14 January 1944.] (5)
31 Jan 1944 Around 0515 A/31, HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) departed from Malta, she then joined convoy GUS 29 around 1045 A/31 for the passage to Gibraltar.
During the passage from Malta to the rendezvous position with the convoy HrMs O 21 was escorted by HMS Falk (T/Lt. W. Metcalfe, RNVR).
[For more info on this convoy see the event ' Convoy GUS 29 ' for 25 January 1944.] (6)
This convoy departed Port Said on 9 May 1944.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was made up of the transports / tankers; Benrinnes (British, 5410 GRT, built 1921), Boskoop (Dutch, 5620 GRT, built 1927), City of Norwich (British, 6726 GRT, built 1913), Elise (Norwegian (tanker), 7910 GRT, built 1931), Empire Ballad (British, 6700 GRT, built 1942), Fort Turtle (British, 7181 GRT, built 1942), Hart Crane (American, 7207 GRT, built 1944) and Modasa (British, 9070 GRT, built 1921).
On departure from Port Said the convoy was escorted by the corvettes HMS Delphinium (Cdr. V.F. Smith, DSO, RD, RNR) and HMS Hyderabad (Lt. G.H. Taylor, RNR).
At 2230 C/9, the Elise and HMS Hyderabad collided between Port Said and Alexandria. HMS Hyderabad put into Alexandria but was later able to rejoin the convoy.

On 10 May 1944 the following transports departed Alexandria to join the convoy; Bialystok (Polish, 7174 GRT, built 1942), Fort Grant (British, 7133 GRT, built 1943), Gorjistan (British, 5888 GRT, built 1929), J.C. Osgood (American, 7207 GRT, built 1944), John A. Brown (British (tanker), 10455 GRT, built 1938), Nordnes (Norwegian, 4147 GRT, built 1932) and Ocean Stranger (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942).
The boom carrier HMS Kirriemoor (4970 GRT, built 1935, Cdr.(Retd.) H.M. Montague, OBE, RN) was also in company with the convoy.

Also the escort destroyer RHS Kriti, frigate HMS Usk (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Pattinson, DSC, RD, RNR), A/S whalers HMSAS Protea (Lt. A. Thomas, DSC, SANF) and HMSAS Southern Sea (Lt.Cdr. M.R.J. Terry-Lloyd, SANF) and A/S trawler HMS Bream (A/Skr.Lt. S.C. Larner, RNR) joined coming from Alexandria.
On 11 May 1944, the Empire Ballad arrived at Alexandria after having been detached due to engine trouble.

On 15 May 1944, the Elise was detached to Malta as was HMS Bream. The transports Samaffric (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944) and Thelma (British, 1593 GRT, built 1935) joined the convoy coming from Malta as did the submarine HMS Trespasser (Lt.Cdr. R.M. Favell, RN). They were escorted to the rendezvous by the A/S whaler HMS Falk ( T/Lt. W. Metcalfe, RNVR). Also on 15 May 1944, the following transports / tankers were detached to Augusta; Bialystok, Boskoop, Fort Grant, Fort Turtle, Hart Crane, J.C. Osgood, John A. Brown, Nordnes and while the following transports joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Domby (British, 5582 GRT, built 1932), Edward Livingston (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Empire Beatrice (British, 7046 GRT, built 1943), Empire Conrad (British, 7009 GRT, built 1942), Empire Noble (British, 7125 GRT, built 1944), Empire Wyclif (British, 6966 GRT, built 1941), Esso Nashville (American (tanker), 7943 GRT, built 1940), Fort de Douaumont (French, 5266 GRT, built 1918), Fort Gaspereau (British, 7134 GRT, built 1943), James Gordon Bennett (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), John B. Hood (American, 7196 GRT, built 1942), John Sullivan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Joseph Le Conte (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Ocean Gallant (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Ocean Traveller (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Richard Bassett (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Samave (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samboston (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samnethy (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Samoa (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sampan (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sampford (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samphire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samwash (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samwye (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Stancleeve (British, 5970 GRT, built 1942), Stanwell (British, 5767 GRT, built 1914), William Few (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942) and William W. Campbell (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943). On 16 May 1944, the transports Edward Livingston and Sampan were detached to Bizerta while the transports Capitaine Paul Lemerle (French, 4945 GRT, built 1925), Empire Derwent (British, 4026 GRT, built 1930), Fort la Baye (British, 7162 GRT, built 1943), Fort Lennox (British, 7149 GRT, built 1943), Harlesden (British, 7273 GRT, built 1943), Josiah Bartlett (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Lornaston (British, 4934 GRT, built 1925), Luther Martin (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942) and Roger Williams (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942) joined coming from Bizerta. Also the AA cruiser HMS Caledon (Capt. R.F. Nichols, RN) and salvage vessel USS Restorer (Lt. C.M. Boyd, USNR) joined the convoy. On 17 May 1944, the transports Capitaine Paul Lemerle, Fort Lennox, Harlesden, Ocean Traveller and Sampford were detached to Bone while the rescue tug HMS Hengist joined the convoy. On 18 May 1944, the transports and tanker Empire Beatrice, Empire Noble, Empire Wyclif, Esso Nashville, Fort de Douaumont, Fort Gaspereau, James Gordon Bennett, John Sullivan, Joseph le Conte, Lornaston, Ocean Gallant, Richard Bassett, Samaye, Samboston, Samoa, Samphire, Samwash, Stancleeve, Thelma, William Few and William W. Campbell as well as USS Restorer arrived at Algiers while the transports / tanker Empire Cavalier (British (tanker), 9891 GRT, built 1942), Grodno (British, 2458 GRT, built 1919), Jared Ingersoll (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), John Sergeant (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Marrakech (French, 6179 GRT, built 1914), Silverelm (British, 4351 GRT, built 1924) and Tabitha Brown (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942) joined the convoy. On 19 May 1944, the transports Empire Conrad, Jared Ingersoll, John Sergeant, Josiah Bertlett, Luther Martin, Roger Williams, Samaffric, Samnethy, Silverelm and Tabitha Brown arrived at Oran having been detached from the Convoy. The transports / tanker Belgian Sailor (Belgian, 7028 GRT, built 1942), Benjamin Tay (British, 1814 GRT, built 1943), Blackheath (British, 4637 GRT, built 1936), Capitaine Saint Martin (French, 3441 GRT, built 1938), Caudebec (French, 1471 GRT, built 1910), Dartmouth (American, 9879 GRT, built 1943), Empire Grebe (British, 5736 GRT, built 1918), Fort Brandon (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Fort la Traite (British, 7134 GRT, built 1942) and Lookout Mountain (American (tanker), 10195 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy coming from Oran. With them were also the LST's USS LST-197 (Lt. J.E. Neal, USNR), USS LST-326 (Lt.(jg) G.R. Ambler, USCGR) and USS LST-381 (Lt. J.P. Houlihan, Jr, USCGR). The convoy arrived at Gibraltar on 20 May 1944.SourcesADM 199/1153 ADM 53/115216 + ADM 199/415ADM 173/17330 ADM 199/728 + ADM 237/244File 2.12.03.6443 (Dutch Archives, The Hague, Netherlands)File 2.12.03.6416 (Dutch Archives, The Hague, Netherlands)ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London. Return to the Allied Warships section

Also on 15 May 1944, the following transports / tankers were detached to Augusta; Bialystok, Boskoop, Fort Grant, Fort Turtle, Hart Crane, J.C. Osgood, John A. Brown, Nordnes and while the following transports joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Domby (British, 5582 GRT, built 1932), Edward Livingston (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Empire Beatrice (British, 7046 GRT, built 1943), Empire Conrad (British, 7009 GRT, built 1942), Empire Noble (British, 7125 GRT, built 1944), Empire Wyclif (British, 6966 GRT, built 1941), Esso Nashville (American (tanker), 7943 GRT, built 1940), Fort de Douaumont (French, 5266 GRT, built 1918), Fort Gaspereau (British, 7134 GRT, built 1943), James Gordon Bennett (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), John B. Hood (American, 7196 GRT, built 1942), John Sullivan (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Joseph Le Conte (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Ocean Gallant (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Ocean Traveller (British, 7178 GRT, built 1942), Richard Bassett (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Samave (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samboston (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samnethy (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Samoa (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sampan (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sampford (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samphire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samwash (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samwye (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Stancleeve (British, 5970 GRT, built 1942), Stanwell (British, 5767 GRT, built 1914), William Few (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942) and William W. Campbell (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943).

On 16 May 1944, the transports Edward Livingston and Sampan were detached to Bizerta while the transports Capitaine Paul Lemerle (French, 4945 GRT, built 1925), Empire Derwent (British, 4026 GRT, built 1930), Fort la Baye (British, 7162 GRT, built 1943), Fort Lennox (British, 7149 GRT, built 1943), Harlesden (British, 7273 GRT, built 1943), Josiah Bartlett (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Lornaston (British, 4934 GRT, built 1925), Luther Martin (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942) and Roger Williams (American, 7177 GRT, built 1942) joined coming from Bizerta. Also the AA cruiser HMS Caledon (Capt. R.F. Nichols, RN) and salvage vessel USS Restorer (Lt. C.M. Boyd, USNR) joined the convoy.
On 17 May 1944, the transports Capitaine Paul Lemerle, Fort Lennox, Harlesden, Ocean Traveller and Sampford were detached to Bone while the rescue tug HMS Hengist joined the convoy.

On 18 May 1944, the transports and tanker Empire Beatrice, Empire Noble, Empire Wyclif, Esso Nashville, Fort de Douaumont, Fort Gaspereau, James Gordon Bennett, John Sullivan, Joseph le Conte, Lornaston, Ocean Gallant, Richard Bassett, Samaye, Samboston, Samoa, Samphire, Samwash, Stancleeve, Thelma, William Few and William W. Campbell as well as USS Restorer arrived at Algiers while the transports / tanker Empire Cavalier (British (tanker), 9891 GRT, built 1942), Grodno (British, 2458 GRT, built 1919), Jared Ingersoll (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), John Sergeant (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Marrakech (French, 6179 GRT, built 1914), Silverelm (British, 4351 GRT, built 1924) and Tabitha Brown (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942) joined the convoy.

On 19 May 1944, the transports Empire Conrad, Jared Ingersoll, John Sergeant, Josiah Bertlett, Luther Martin, Roger Williams, Samaffric, Samnethy, Silverelm and Tabitha Brown arrived at Oran having been detached from the Convoy. The transports / tanker Belgian Sailor (Belgian, 7028 GRT, built 1942), Benjamin Tay (British, 1814 GRT, built 1943), Blackheath (British, 4637 GRT, built 1936), Capitaine Saint Martin (French, 3441 GRT, built 1938), Caudebec (French, 1471 GRT, built 1910), Dartmouth (American, 9879 GRT, built 1943), Empire Grebe (British, 5736 GRT, built 1918), Fort Brandon (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Fort la Traite (British, 7134 GRT, built 1942) and Lookout Mountain (American (tanker), 10195 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy coming from Oran. With them were also the LST's USS LST-197 (Lt. J.E. Neal, USNR), USS LST-326 (Lt.(jg) G.R. Ambler, USCGR) and USS LST-381 (Lt. J.P. Houlihan, Jr, USCGR).
The convoy arrived at Gibraltar on 20 May 1944.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
